question.title: How many more (or fewer) seats will SDS win compared to GS in the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary election?
question.description:
The two largest parties in the run-up to the March 22, 2026 Slovenian parliamentary election are [Janez Janša](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Janez_Jan%C5%A1a)'s [Slovenian Democratic Party](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slovenian_Democratic_Party) (SDS) and [Robert Golob](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Golob)'s [Freedom Movement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freedom_Movement_\(Slovenia\)) (GS), the latter having led the governing coalition since 2022. 

[Opinion polls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling_for_the_2026_Slovenian_parliamentary_election) in early 2026 have generally shown a small lead for SDS, with a substantial share of undecided respondents. In Slovenia, governments are typically coalitions, and the relative strength of SDS and GS will impact the likelihood of them leading the governing coalition. Since 1992, the party with the most seats has been excluded from the governing coalition only 2 out of 9 times.

The past year was marked by several developments that have strengthened SDS in the run-up to the election. In April 2025, Janez Janša [was acquitted](https://www.reuters.com/world/court-acquits-slovenias-ex-pm-jansa-corruption-charges-2025-04-18/) of corruption-related charges in a case he had characterised as politically motivated. In November 2025, a [binding referendum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_Slovenian_assisted_dying_referendum) rejected legislation to legalise assisted dying, which was [characterised by international coverage](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/majority-slovenians-voted-against-law-assisted-dying-2025-11-23/) as a setback for the governing coalition and evidence of effective mobilisation by conservative opposition groups. In February 2026, the National Assembly finally [approved a new Governor of the Bank of Slovenia](https://www.ft.com/content/923e3da2-eceb-4351-a8c6-7201257ed142) following a year-long vacancy.

question.resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the number of seats won by the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) minus the number of seats won by the Freedom Movement (GS) in the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary election.
question.fine_print:
If the final results are not known before May 1, 2026, this question will resolve based on provisional results or may be annulled.

However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled.
